name: Oysters Rockefeller (Gourmet Mag.)
recipeIngredient: 1 Boston lettuce, washed, dried and chopped fine
1/2 lb Fresh spinach, washed, dried and chopped fine
recipeInstructions:
1. Shuck oysters and reserve the liquor and bottom shells.
2. Scrub and dry the shells.
3. In a bowl, combine lettuce, spinach, scallion, 1/2 cup bread crumbs, parsley, celery, and garlic.
4. In a skillet, melt butter over moderate heat and cook the spinach mixture, stirring for 1-2 minutes or until greens are wilted. Stir in Pernod, anchovy paste, cayenne, and salt and pepper to taste. Chill the mixture, covered, for 1 hour.
5. Cook bacon in another skillet over moderate heat until crisp. Transfer to paper towels to drain, then crumble it.
6. Arrange one oyster in each of the reserved shells and moisten with some of the reserved liquor.
7. Spread half the spinach mixture by heaping tablespoons onto the oysters.
8. Sprinkle bacon over each oyster.
9. Top the bacon with remaining spinach mixture and sprinkle each with 1 tablespoon of the remaining bread crumbs.
10. Arrange oysters on an oven-proof platter filled with coarse salt.
11. Bake in a preheated 450°F oven for 18 minutes or until the bread crumbs are well browned.
12. Garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon wedges, then serve.
